records.ety: [From Galvani: cf. F. galvanisme. See Galvanic.]

defs: Electricity excited by the mutual action of certain liquids and metals; dynamical electricity.
The branch of physical science which treats of dynamical electricity, or the properties and effects of electrical currents.
notes: ☞ The words galvanism and galvanic, formerly in very general use, are now rarely employed. For the latter, voltaic, from the name of Volta, is commonly used.
